sections.paragraph: m. pr. something mixed, specially meslin, provender consisting of several kinds of grain, as wheat, barley, vetches, and other seeds (comp. Varro, De R. R. i. 31; Plin. xviii. 15, s. 41), all of which were sown mixed together [“or given to cattle”], Job 6:5 24:6 . It is clear that grain is to be understood from Isa 30:24.
